title: 09 - General Pathology of Infectious Diseases - Exam Prep Questions
description: Third-party generated single-choice exam-prep questions for Chapter 9. The set is aligned primarily to the Chapter 9 textbook content and shaped to reflect the style, structure, and difficulty distribution of the RCPA Basic Pathological Sciences mock exam format. Choices are keyed so answers remain stable if an application randomizes display order.

disclaimer.endorsement_notice: These questions are third-party generated study materials. They are not produced by, reviewed by, approved by, endorsed by, or affiliated with the textbook authors, Elsevier, the Royal College of Pathologists of Australasia, or any other authority, institution, publisher, or examining body.
disclaimer.answer_key_note: Answers are included as stable choice keys with brief source-grounded rationales for study and review purposes.

source_notes.reference_note: Used as a style, structure, and difficulty reference.

questions.question: What change makes prion diseases transmissible?

choices.text: Abnormal protease-resistant PrP converts normal PrP into the abnormal form
Integration of prion RNA into the host genome
Production of bacterial endotoxin within neurons
Loss of host ribosomes in the cerebral cortex

answer.reason: Pathogenic prion disease results when abnormal protease-resistant PrP promotes conversion of normal protease-sensitive PrP into the abnormal form.

questions.question:
A virus infects only cells that express a particular surface glycoprotein and spares nearby receptor-negative cells.

What MOST directly explains this tissue tropism?

answer.reason: Viral tropism is strongly influenced by specific host cell receptors that allow attachment and entry into target cells.

questions.question:
Lung biopsy shows enlarged cells with a large eosinophilic nuclear inclusion and smaller basophilic cytoplasmic inclusions.

Which virus is MOST strongly suggested by this morphology?

answer.reason: CMV infection characteristically produces enlarged cells with a prominent eosinophilic nuclear inclusion and smaller basophilic cytoplasmic inclusions.

questions.question:
A patient with gram-negative sepsis develops hypotension and disseminated intravascular coagulation.

Which bacterial component is MOST directly responsible for triggering this host response?

answer.reason: Lipid A of gram-negative LPS activates CD14 and TLR4, driving cytokine release that can culminate in septic shock and DIC.

questions.question:
A yeast-like organism in tissue has a capsule highlighted by mucicarmine stain.

Which pathogen is MOST consistent with this finding?

answer.reason: Mucicarmine is a classic stain for cryptococci because it highlights the organism's capsule.

questions.question:
A patient develops toxin-mediated colitis after broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy.

What change MOST likely permitted this infection to develop?

choices.text: Loss of normal commensal flora with dysbiosis

answer.reason: Antibiotics can disrupt the normal intestinal microbiome, allowing toxin-producing C. difficile to overgrow and cause disease.

questions.question:
A patient with fever, altered consciousness, and temporal lobe abnormalities is suspected of having herpes simplex encephalitis. Cerebrospinal fluid viral culture is negative.

What is the BEST next diagnostic test?

choices.text: CSF polymerase chain reaction for HSV

answer.reason: PCR testing of CSF is far more sensitive than viral culture for HSV encephalitis and is the preferred diagnostic test.

questions.question:
During an outbreak, a new Escherichia coli strain is found to have acquired shiga-toxin production after genetic exchange with a bacteriophage.

What mechanism BEST explains the emergence of this more virulent strain?

choices.text: Antigenic drift within a capsule locus
Bacteriophage-mediated transfer of a virulence gene

answer.reason: The chapter specifically cites acquisition of shiga-toxin genes from a bacteriophage as a mechanism by which new virulent E. coli strains emerge.

questions.question: Which agent is classified as a CDC category A bioterrorism threat because it is readily transmitted person to person, has high mortality, and lacks reliably effective therapy?

answer.reason: Smallpox is specifically highlighted as a category A agent because of high transmissibility, major public-health impact, and high mortality.

questions.question:
A volunteer study shows that Vibrio cholerae infection requires a very large inoculum in healthy individuals, but the infectious dose falls dramatically when stomach acid is neutralized.

Which host defense is MOST responsible for this protection?

answer.reason: Acidic gastric secretions are a key defense against many GI pathogens, and cholera requires a much smaller inoculum when that barrier is neutralized.

questions.question:
Several enteric pathogens gain access to gut-associated lymphoid tissue by crossing specialized epithelial cells overlying mucosal lymphoid aggregates.

Which cells are being exploited?

answer.reason: M cells overlying mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue are important entry points for several GI pathogens, including poliovirus and some enteric bacteria.

questions.question:
An elderly patient develops Streptococcus pneumoniae pneumonia shortly after recovering from influenza.

What change caused by the viral infection MOST likely predisposed to this bacterial superinfection?

choices.text: Reduced complement synthesis in the liver

answer.reason: Influenza can damage the ciliated respiratory epithelium, weakening mucociliary defenses and predisposing to secondary bacterial pneumonia.

questions.question:
Several weeks after a dog bite, a patient develops progressive encephalitis.

By what route does rabies virus MOST characteristically reach the brain?

answer.reason: Rabies reaches the CNS by retrograde transport along nerves from the site of inoculation.

questions.question:
A systemic mycosis is cultured as hyphae at room temperature but as yeast at body temperature.

What term BEST describes this fungal behavior?

answer.reason: Several important pathogenic fungi are thermally dimorphic, growing as hyphae at room temperature and as yeast forms at body temperature.

questions.question:
A patient with acute hepatitis B has marked hepatocyte injury despite relatively little direct viral cytopathic effect.

What mechanism is MOST responsible for the liver cell damage?

choices.text: Cytotoxic T-cell-mediated destruction of infected hepatocytes

answer.reason: HBV liver injury is largely caused by CTL-mediated destruction of infected hepatocytes rather than by a major direct cytopathic viral effect.

questions.question: Why does rhinovirus infection usually remain confined to the upper respiratory tract?

choices.text: It can replicate only inside erythrocytes
It requires bile salts for entry
It replicates best at the lower temperature of the upper airway
It uses M cells to reach Peyer patches

answer.reason: Rhinoviruses show tissue tropism for the upper airway because they replicate best at the relatively cooler temperatures found there.

questions.question:
A patient with inhalational anthrax develops marked soft-tissue edema.

What is the primary action of anthrax edema factor?

choices.text: Calmodulin-dependent adenylate cyclase activity that increases cAMP

answer.reason: Anthrax edema factor acts as a calmodulin-dependent adenylate cyclase, increasing intracellular cAMP and causing edema.

questions.question:
A patient with fever, hypotension, diffuse erythema, and multiorgan dysfunction is found to have Staphylococcus aureus producing a superantigen.

What is the MOST important pathogenic effect of this toxin?

choices.text: Blockade of acetylcholine release at the neuromuscular junction
Massive nonspecific T-cell activation with cytokine release
Neutralization of secretory IgA in the gut

answer.reason: Superantigens activate very large numbers of T cells, producing a cytokine surge that drives toxic shock syndrome.

questions.question:
A strain of uropathogenic Escherichia coli persists in the urinary tract because it strongly adheres to uroepithelial cells.

Which virulence factor is MOST directly responsible?

answer.reason: Uropathogenic E. coli use P pili to bind Gal(alpha1-4)Gal moieties on uroepithelial cells, promoting urinary tract colonization.

questions.question:
A lung biopsy shows granulomas with epithelioid macrophages, giant cells, and central caseous necrosis.

What host response MOST strongly underlies this pattern?

choices.text: T-cell-mediated delayed hypersensitivity with macrophage activation

answer.reason: Granulomatous inflammation with caseation is driven by strong T-cell-mediated immunity that activates macrophages against difficult-to-eradicate organisms such as M. tuberculosis.

questions.question:
Streptococcus pneumoniae has more than 90 serotypes based on differences in capsular polysaccharide.

What immune-evasion strategy does this BEST illustrate?

answer.reason: Differences in capsular serotypes help pneumococci escape preexisting neutralizing and opsonizing antibody responses.

questions.question:
Mycobacterium tuberculosis survives within macrophages after phagocytosis.

Which mechanism BEST explains this intracellular persistence?

answer.reason: M. tuberculosis avoids intracellular killing by preventing normal fusion of the phagosome with the lysosome.

questions.question:
A DNA virus reduces recognition of infected cells by CD8+ T lymphocytes.

Which host pathway is MOST likely being targeted?

choices.text: Antigen presentation by MHC class I molecules

answer.reason: Several DNA viruses evade CD8+ T-cell recognition by altering or reducing MHC class I expression and antigen presentation.

questions.question:
A microbiology laboratory uses a proteomic method to rapidly identify bacterial species from cultured isolates, but it still relies on culture-based methods for antimicrobial susceptibility testing.

Which method is being described?

answer.reason: Mass spectrometry can rapidly identify organisms by protein profile, but it does not replace culture for antibiotic sensitivity testing.

questions.question:
A cervical lesion caused by human papillomavirus shows epithelial proliferation with relatively sparse inflammation.

Which pattern of tissue response BEST fits this lesion?

answer.reason: Many viral lesions, including those caused by HPV, produce a cytopathic-cytoproliferative pattern with epithelial proliferation and limited inflammation.

questions.question:
A patient with clostridial myonecrosis has extensive muscle destruction with comparatively little inflammatory infiltrate.

What pathologic mechanism BEST explains this morphology?

answer.reason: Clostridial infections can cause such severe toxin-mediated necrosis that tissue destruction dominates over the inflammatory infiltrate.

questions.question:
A patient with AIDS has a duodenal biopsy showing macrophages packed with acid-fast bacilli but no granulomatous response.

What defect BEST explains the absence of granuloma formation?

answer.reason: Granuloma formation depends on effective T-cell-mediated immunity, which is profoundly impaired in AIDS.

questions.question: A child with X-linked agammaglobulinemia is MOST likely to develop severe infections with which group of pathogens?

answer.reason: Antibody deficiency most strongly predisposes to severe infections with extracellular bacteria, especially encapsulated organisms.

questions.question:
A young adult with recurrent meningococcal infections is found to have a complement deficiency.

Which part of the complement system is MOST likely deficient?

choices.text: Late complement components involved in membrane attack complex formation

answer.reason: Deficiencies of the late complement components are classically associated with recurrent Neisseria infections.

questions.question:
Chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis is associated with inherited defects in IL-17 immunity, including some STAT3 abnormalities.

What arm of host defense is MOST directly impaired?

answer.reason: IL-17 and Th17 pathways are important for mucocutaneous antifungal defense, and their disruption predisposes to chronic candidiasis.
